docs.title: Introduction
docs.text:
Discord is a free communication platform that allows users to send messages, share files, and host voice and video calls. This guide provides step-by-step instructions for setting up and using Discord for team collaboration in the CST program.

For more information about Discord's full range of features, visit the Discord website.

docs.title: Is This Guide for You?
docs.text: This guide is written for term 1 CST students who have no prior experience with Discord. The instructions work on any operating system, including macOS and Windows.

docs.title: By the End of This Guide, You Will Be Able To
docs.text:
1. Create an account, add friends, and start chatting
  2. Configure Server Settings and Access
  3. Organize Channels and Set Posting Permissions
  4. Host a team meeting using Discord

docs.title: Prerequisites
docs.text:
Before you begin, make sure you have the following:

  • A computer running macOS or Windows
  • A valid email address
  • A stable internet connection
  • Basic familiarity with mouse and keyboard operations

docs.title: Typographical Conventions
docs.text:
This guide uses the following typographic conventions:

Convention Description Example Commands & Actions Bolded verbs indicate actions you must perform Click, Type, Select, Press [Menu & Button Sequence] Square brackets indicate menus or buttons. The → symbol shows the order of clicks [User Settings] → [Voice & Video] → [Save Changes] Keyboard Keys Monospace text indicates keys to press Enter, Ctrl, Shift

docs.title: Admonitions
docs.text:
This guide uses three types of messages to provide additional information:

Success

A Success message describes the expected outcome of a completed step.

Warning

A Warning message highlights an action that may cause an error or unexpected result.

Note

A Note provides helpful context or extra detail for a step.

docs.title: Glossary
docs.text: Term Definition Channel A dedicated space within a server for text, voice, or video communication. Community Server A server type with additional safety and moderation features enabled. Friend Request An invitation sent to another user to connect on Discord. Invite Link A shareable URL that allows others to join a specific server. Permission A setting controlling what actions a user can perform in a server or channel. Role A label assigned to members that groups them by access level or function. Server A shared workspace containing channels, members, and settings. Username A unique identifier for each Discord account used to find and add friends. Voice Channel A channel for real-time audio, video, and screen sharing communication. @mention A way to notify a specific user by typing @ followed by their username.

docs.title: Troubleshooting Guide
docs.text: Problem Possible Cause Solution You did not receive the verification email after creating your account. The email may have gone to your spam or junk folder, or you entered the wrong email address. Check your spam or junk folder. If the email is not there, return to Discord and click [Resend Email]. Make sure your email address is entered correctly. You cannot find your classmate when sending a friend request. The username was entered incorrectly, or the user has privacy settings that restrict friend requests. Double-check the exact username with your classmate, including capitalization. Ask them to send the friend request to you instead. You cannot hear others during a voice meeting. Your output device may be incorrectly configured in Discord, or your speaker/headphone volume is too low. Go to [User Settings] → [Voice & Video] and check that the correct output device is selected. Also check your system volume. Others cannot hear you during a voice meeting. Your microphone may be muted in Discord, or the wrong input device is selected. Check that the microphone icon in the voice channel is not crossed out. Go to [User Settings] → [Voice & Video] and confirm the correct input device is selected. The invite link is not working for your teammates. The link may have expired or reached its maximum number of uses. Return to [Server Settings] → [Invites] and generate a new invite link. Make sure to set an appropriate expiration time and usage limit. You cannot create a text/voice channel or change server settings. You may not have the required permissions in the server. Contact the server owner to grant you the appropriate role or permissions. Your screen share is lagging or freezing during the meeting. Your internet connection may be unstable, or too many applications are running. Close unnecessary applications to free up memory. Move closer to your Wi-Fi router or switch to a wired connection if possible.

docs.title: Introduction
docs.text:
In this section, you will create and organize text/voice channels, then configure permissions so the right people can post in the right places.

A clear channel structure reduces noise, keeps project communication organized, and helps team members find information quickly.

docs.title: Prerequisites
docs.text:
• You have already completed server setup (Task 2).
  • You have permission to manage channels in the server.
  • Your team has agreed on basic communication rules.

Note

This channel structure is optimized for team collaboration, not large public communities.

docs.title: Recommended Channel Structure (3–10 Member Team)
docs.text:
• announcements (important updates only)
  • general (daily team chat)
  • meeting-agenda (topics before meetings)
  • meeting-minutes (notes after meetings)
  • resources (shared links/files)
  • Team Meeting Room (voice channel)

docs.title: Setup Steps
docs.text:
1. After you Enable [Community], Discord automatically creates [#rules] and [#moderator-only].
    • [#rules]: By default, this channel appears at the top with a special header. You can post your server rules and team meeting agreement here.
    • [#moderator-only]: Discord sends Community-related updates here for admins/moderators. Some information may be sensitive, so only the server owner can view it by default.

Note

These two channels cannot be directly deleted at first. You must first reassign their Community responsibilities to other channels, then remove channels that no longer hold those roles.

  1. To create a new text channel, hover over [Text Channels] and Click [+].

Note

If you have any problems with creating channel, please check Troubleshooting.

  1. Select [Text], Type a [Channel Name] (for example, "announcements"), then Click [Create Channel].

  2. To create a voice channel, hover over [Voice Channels] and Click [+].

  3. Select [Voice], Type a [Channel Name] (for example, "Team Meeting Room"), then Click [Create Channel]. Why: This gives your team a dedicated space for live discussion.

  4. To edit a channel, hover over the channel, right-click, and Select [Edit Channel]. Why: For example, you may want to limit posting permissions in [#announcements].

  5. In [Overview], Type a short description in [Channel Topic] to explain the channel purpose. Why: Clear channel purpose reduces confusion and improves communication consistency.

  6. In [Permissions], Click [Advanced Permissions].

  7. Under [ROLES/MEMBERS], confirm [@everyone] is selected. Then scroll to [Text Channel Permissions] → [Send Messages] and Click the red [X] to deny posting for everyone. See this first: Click this:

  8. Scroll up and Click [+] next to [ROLES/MEMBERS]. When members appear, Select a specific member (or role). Click [+]: Member list appears:

  9. Scroll down to [Send Messages], then Click the green [✓] to allow posting for that selected member/role. Click [Save Changes], then Press [Esc] (top-right) to exit [Edit Channel]. Exit [Edit Channel]:

  10. If you want to remove a channel, hover over it, right-click, and Click [Delete Channel].

Success

Core channel management training is complete and ready for team collaboration.

docs.title: Conclusion
docs.text: Your Discord channel setup is now complete and structured for effective team collaboration. You have configured channel types, channel purpose descriptions, and permissions so communication stays clear, organized, and role-appropriate.

docs.title: Introduction
docs.text: Discord allows users to host voice or video meetings using voice channels. This guide will walk you through how to start and manage a team meeting effectively.

docs.title: Prerequisites
docs.text:
Before starting, make sure you have:

  • A Discord server
  • Permission to use your microphone and camera

docs.title: Start a Meeting
docs.text:
After joining a server, you can host a meeting with your classmates or teammates. This section will show you how to start a team meeting.

  1. The left sidebar displays all the servers you’ve joined. Click the server where you want to host your meeting.

  2. Select a voice channel where you want to start the meeting.

  3. Click the [Invite to Voice] icon to invite your classmates

    Warning

    Clicking anywhere else in the channel will immediately start a voice meeting.

  4. Choose who you want to invite and click [Invite], or share the invite link with your classmates.

    Note

    If you have any problems with invite link, please check troubleshooting guide

  5. Click the voice channel to begin the meeting.

  6. You are now in a voice meeting.

    Note

    If you have any problems with sound, please check troubleshooting guide

    Voice meeting started

    You have successfully start a voice meeting.

  7. Click the [screen share] icon to present your screen to others.

    Note

    If you have any problems with sharing screen, please check troubleshooting guide

  8. Click [camera] icon to turn on your camera.

  9. Once your camera is on, your meeting becomes a video meeting.

    Success

    You have successfully start a video meeting.

  10. Use the [microphone] icon to mute or unmute yourself.

  11. Click the [red disconnect] button to leave and end the meeting.

docs.title: Conclusion
docs.text:
You have now learned how to:

  • Start a voice meeting
  • Invite participants
  • Use screen sharing and video
  • Manage audio controls
  • End a meeting

You’re ready to host a team meeting and collaborate with your teammates on Discord!

docs.title: Create and Configure a Discord Server
Introduction
docs.text:
In this section, you will configure the server-level settings your team depends on: server name, privacy/safety options, and invite access.

A properly configured server creates a stable foundation for collaboration before channels and meetings are set up.

docs.title: Prerequisites
docs.text:
• You have a Discord account and are signed in.
  • You are using the Discord desktop app or web version.
  • Your team size is 3–10 members.

Note

This setup is optimized for private team collaboration, not public community servers. Public community servers require stricter safety and moderation configuration.

docs.title: Recommended Configuration (3–10 Member Team)
docs.text:
• Default Notifications: Only @mentions
  • Invite Expiration: 7 days
  • Invite Max Uses: 10–20
  • Safety Filters: Enabled

docs.title: Setup Steps
docs.text:
1. Open Discord and Click [+ (Add a Server)].

  2. Select [Create My Own] and Choose the server type. Why: This creates a custom server suited to your team’s use case.

  3. Choose the server purpose. If you are unsure, you can skip this question.

  4. Type a clear server name and optionally add an icon, then Click [Create]. Why: A clear name helps teammates identify the correct server quickly.

  5. Open the server dropdown and Click [Server Settings]. Why: All core configuration options are managed in [Server Settings]. The dropdown menu should appear as shown below.

  6. In [Server Settings], scroll to the bottom of the left sidebar and Click [Enable Community]. On the right side, Click [Enable Community] again to start setup. Why: Community setup enables useful management and baseline safety features.

  7. In [Community Setup], complete [Safety Checks], Check [Scan media content from all members], then Click [Next]. Why: These filters reduce inappropriate content and keep the collaboration space clean.

  8. In [Community Setup], Discord may auto-skip [Setting Up the Basics] by applying defaults (for example, Mentions Only notifications and disabling risky permissions for @everyone). When you reach [Finish Touches], Check [I agree and understand], then Click [Finish Setup]. Why: This reduces notification overload while preserving important alerts.

  9. After setup completes, you may hear a Discord notification sound. This is normal; you can review notifications later. Then Click [Invites] in the left sidebar.

  10. In [Invites], find [Create Invite Link] on the right side and Click it.

  11. In the pop-up window, choose one option:

    • Click [Invite] to let Discord send an invite directly.
    • Click [Copy] to copy the invite link and send it manually to your teammate.

Success

Core server setup is complete and ready for your next tasks.

docs.title: Conclusion
docs.text: Your Discord server is now configured for team collaboration with appropriate identity, safety, notification, and invite settings for a 3–10 member team.

docs.title: Introduction
docs.text: Discord is a communications platform that enables you to communicate with lecturers and classmates during your time in BCIT. This guide explains how to create an account, add friends and start your first conversation.

docs.title: Prerequisites
docs.text:
• You have an email account.
  • You have installed the Discord application.

docs.title: Create An Account
docs.text:
To use Discord, you must first create an account. This section will show you how to register a free Discord account.

  1. Click [Register] to create a new account.

  2. Enter your email address, username, password, date of birth and click [Create Account] button.

  3. Your account is created, and you will be directed to the main page.

Note

You can verify your email now or later by clicking the link sent to your email. If you have any problems with verification, please check troubleshooting guide

Success

Your Discord account has been successfully created.

docs.title: Add Friend
docs.text: After creating an account, you can add your classmates to start a conversation with them. This section will show you how to add friend in Discord.

docs.title: Send Request
docs.text:
1. Click [Friends] button direct to friends manage page.

  2. Click [Add Friend] button.

  3. Enter the username of your friends and click [Send Friend Request] button.

!!! note If you have any problems with sending friend request, please check troubleshooting guide

  1. Click [Pending] button to view all the pending request you send.

  2. Click [All] button to view list of your friend.

Success

Your friend request has been successfully sent.

docs.title: Receive Request
docs.text:
1. Click [Friends] button direct to friends manage page.

  2. Click [Pending] button to view all the pending request.

  3. Click [Accept] button if you want to accept request.

  4. Click [Accept] button again to confirm friend request.

  5. Friend added and direct to the friend list.

Success

You have successfully accepted the friend request.

docs.title: Start Chatting
docs.text:
After adding a friend, you can start your first conversation with them. This section will show you how to start chatting with your friend.

  1. Click [All] button and choose the friend you want to communicate with and click user.

  2. Enter your message and click Enter to send your message.

Success

You have successfully started your first chat on Discord.

docs.title: Conclusion
docs.text:
In this section, you have learned how to:

  • Create a Discord account
  • Add friends
  • Send and receive friend requests
  • Start a conversation

You are now ready to use Discord for communication.
